SYSTEM CONTEXT & BUSINESS STRATEGY: OSCAR SANCHEZ WEDDING PHOTOGRAPHY
Please read the following business strategy, ideal client psychological profile, and UX/Conversion funnel carefully. You must use this context as your core lens whenever you write code, design UI components, or draft copywriting for my Next.js website.

1. BRAND IDENTITY & MARKET POSITIONING
Niche: High-end / Premium Wedding Photography.
Location: Ciudad Juárez, Mexico (Exclusive focus).
Style: Cinematic, editorial, and 100% documentary.
Brand Archetype: The "Director of Peace of Mind". I take control of the visual logistics so the couple can completely let go and enjoy their party without stress.
Language & Tone: Spanish (Mexico). Elegant, empathetic, premium, and reassuring. MUST have impeccable grammar and accents (tildes). Never transactional.
2. OUR CORE PROMISE (UNIQUE SELLING PROPOSITION)
The Ultimate Promise: "El pastel se terminará, pero tu historia merece vivirse sin estrés y recordarse con estilo. Fotografía cinematográfica en Ciudad Juárez que captura la verdadera esencia de su conexión, mientras ustedes disfrutan cada segundo de su evento."
(Translation: The cake will be eaten, but your story deserves to be lived without stress and remembered with style. Cinematic photography that captures your true connection while you enjoy every second of your event.)

3. BUYER PERSONAS & PSYCHOLOGICAL TRIGGERS
We have two main decision-makers. Our copy and UX must speak to both simultaneously:

A. The Bride (Triggers: Transcendence, Aesthetics, Security)

Her desires: A visual legacy. She planned the aesthetics for months and wants photos that look like a movie (editorial/cinematic).
Her fears ("The Mannequin Syndrome"): Terrified of looking awkward, stiff, or overly posed. She needs to know she will look naturally beautiful.
B. The Groom (Triggers: Freedom, Control, Efficiency)

His desires: Clear process, zero hidden fees, and high efficiency.
His fears ("The Party Hijacking"): He hates the idea of being pulled away from his friends for forced poses. He paid for a party to drink, dance, and celebrate, not to work as a model.
4. THE FUNNEL & UX STRATEGY (CRITICAL)
Do NOT build generic e-commerce flows. We use a High-End Lead Generation Funnel:

The "Mid-Range Anchor" Strategy: On the public website, we ONLY show the "Clásica" collection ($10,500 MXN) as the featured/popular package. We do NOT show the $14,500 MXN or $7,000 MXN packages directly on the UI. This filters out low-budget leads and eliminates decision fatigue.
Primary Call to Action (CTA): "Solicitar Guía de Inversión Completa" (Request Full Investment Guide). The goal of the website is NOT to sell a package instantly, but to get them to click this button.
Value-Based Lead Qualification: The CTA leads to a contact form. We NEVER ask for "Budget" because it causes friction for premium clients. Instead, we ask: "¿Qué es lo más importante para ustedes en sus fotos?" (What is most important to you in your photos?).
The WhatsApp Handoff: The form does not send an email; it generates a pre-filled WhatsApp message. Once they text me, I send them the full PDF Guide (which uses the $14,500 Signature package as the top anchor, making the $10,500 package they saw on the web an absolute no-brainer).
YOUR DIRECTIVE AS AN AI ASSISTANT:
Whenever I ask you to create or audit a component, check it against these rules:

Does the copy reflect the "Zero Stress / Total Freedom" promise?
Are we avoiding cheap/transactional words like "comprar" (buy) or "barato" (cheap), using "invertir" (invest) and "colección" (collection) instead?
Is the UI focused on driving users to the "Solicitar Guía" WhatsApp funnel?
Are all Spanish accents and special characters flawlessly implemented in the JSON/Code?
If you understand this business logic, acknowledge it and wait for my first specific request.
